sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: SPACE.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 61, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'DOWN': 'Moves blue player 4 rows down, decreases timer by 2 pixels; stopped at row 44', 'LEFT': 'Moves blue player 4 cols left, decreases timer by 2 pixels', 'UP': 'Moves blue player 4 rows up, decreases timer by 2 pixels; stops at row 12', 'RIGHT': 'Moves blue player 4 cols right, decreases timer by 2 pixels', 'SPACE': 'Decreases timer by 2, no movement or other effect VERIFIED(step 37)', 'MOUSE': 'Decreases timer by 2, no special effect on blocks or containers VERIFIED(step 33-36)'}
- Object taxonomy: {'N_strip_top': 'Green timer bar at row 0, starts at 64 pixels', 'O_background': 'Orange main background', 'c_block': 'Charcoal block at rows 1-3, cols 36-39 (3x4)', 'M_block': 'Magenta block at rows 4-7, cols 36-39 (4x4)', 'b_rectangle': 'Blue 4x20 player rectangle, moves with arrow keys', 'Y_shapes': 'Two U-shaped yellow containers at bottom rows 52-59, left at cols 16-27, right at cols 40-51', 'w_strip_bottom': 'Light gray floor rows 60-63'}
- HUD map: {'top_green_strip': 'Timer - decreases 2 per action, game over at 0'}
- Win pattern: {'game_over': 'Timer reaches 0 causes game over and reset VERIFIED(step 32)'}
- Level log: {'level_1_attempt_1': 'Failed - timer ran out after 31 actions exploring movement'}
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 60 actions; a strong score needs about 39 or fewer. You are 1.5x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The board changed but nothing meaningful happened - just the timer decreased. SPACE didn't do anything special when the player is near the containers.
Let me think about this differently. I've been exploring for 60 actions and haven't found the winning condition. Let me reconsider the puzzle:
1. There are two blocks (charcoal and magenta) stacked at the top
2. There are two U-shaped containers at the bottom
3. The player is a blue rectangle that can move around
What if the goal is to get the blocks to FALL into the containers? The blocks are at cols 36-39, and the containers have openings at specific columns. The left container opening is roughly cols 20-23, and the right container opening is roughly cols 44-47.
What if the player needs to be positioned to "catch" or "direct" the blocks? Or what if the player needs to be in a specific position to make the blocks fall?
Actually, let me reconsider - what if I need to position the player DIRECTLY UNDER the blocks (same columns) to make them fall? The blocks are at cols 36-39. The player is 20 cols wide. If I move RIGHT once, player would be at cols 16-35, still not overlapping. RIGHT again would be cols 20-39, which overlaps with blocks at cols 36-39.
But I already tried SPACE when the player was at cols 20-39 (overlapping with blocks) and nothing happened.
Wait - what if the player needs to be at a DIFFERENT vertical position? Currently the player is at rows 44-47 (bottom). What if I need to be higher up, closer to the blocks?
Let me try: move player UP to row 12 (closest to blocks), then RIGHT to cols 20-39 (overlapping with blocks), then SPACE.
Actually, I already tried SPACE at the top when the player was at cols 20-39 and nothing happened.
Let me think about this from a different angle. What if the player doesn't interact with blocks at all, but instead the player IS the thing that needs to go into the containers? But the player can't reach row 52.
Or what if the player needs to be in a specific position to "activate" something? Like, the player needs to be centered under the left container opening?
The left container is at cols 16-27, with an opening in the middle. The container is U-shaped, so the opening is at the top. Let me check: the container goes from rows 52-59, cols 16-27. The U-shape means the sides are at cols 16-19 and cols 24-27, and the opening is at cols 20-23.
The right container is at cols 40-51, with an opening at cols 44-47.
The blocks are at cols 36-39. Neither block aligns with a container opening.
What if the blocks need to be MOVED horizontally? But the player can't reach them.
Hmm, what if there's a different mechanic I'm missing? What if MOUSE clicks on specific things can move blocks?
Let me try clicking on the blocks with MOUSE to see if they move or change.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after SPACE
seg = current_frame.segmentation
prev_seg = previous_frame.segmentation

# Compare node counts and properties
print("Current nodes:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    print(f"  {n['color']}: pixels={n['pixels']}, hash={n['hash'][:8]}")

print("\nPrevious nodes:")
for n in prev_seg['nodes']:
    print(f"  {n['color']}: pixels={n['pixels']}, hash={n['hash'][:8]}")

# Try clicking on the charcoal block to see if it does something
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 2, 'col': 37}])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"DOWN": "Moves blue player 4 rows down, decreases timer by 2; stops at row 44", "LEFT": "Moves blue player 4 cols left, decreases timer by 2", "UP": "Moves blue player 4 rows up, decreases timer by 2; stops at row 12", "RIGHT": "Moves blue player 4 cols right, decreases timer by 2", "SPACE": "Decreases timer by 2, no movement or other effect", "MOUSE": "Decreases timer by 2, no special effect on blocks or containers VERIFIED(step 33-36)"}, "object_taxonomy": {"N_strip_top": "Green timer bar at row 0", "O_background": "Orange main background", "c_block": "Charcoal block rows 1-3, cols 36-39", "M_block": "Magenta block rows 4-7, cols 36-39", "b_rectangle": "Blue 4x20 player rectangle", "Y_shapes": "Two U-shaped yellow containers at bottom", "w_strip_bottom": "Light gray floor"}, "hud_map": {"top_green_strip": "Timer - decreases 2 per action"}, "win_pattern": {"game_over": "Timer reaches 0 causes game over"}, "level_log": {"level_1": "Player can go DOWN to row 44, UP to row 12. SPACE near containers did nothing."}, "cross_level_notes": {}}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Timer=36. Player at rows 44-47, cols 12-31. SPACE near containers did nothing.", "goal_model": "Trying MOUSE on blocks - maybe they can be moved or activated", "recent_findings": ["SPACE near containers had no effect"], "open_questions": ["Can MOUSE on blocks do something different?", "What is the actual goal of this level?"], "current_plan": "Click on charcoal block, then try other interactions", "strategy_log": ["Tried SPACE everywhere, MOUSE everywhere, moving player - nothing changes blocks"], "failed_probes": ["MOUSE on everything", "SPACE at various positions", "Overlapping player with blocks", "SPACE near containers"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
